subject: Updated: Beginner class classification spec — Harvey input merged
body:
AIDI — spec updated to v2. Harvey's response is fully merged.

Doc: Collab/architecture/beginner-class-classification-spec.md

What changed since v1:
- Critical evaluation order codified (negatives → mixed → positives → desc fallback)
- Edge cases added Harvey caught: Beyond Beginner, Ongoing Beginner, Tango 100S/100M (danceus), Beginner+, Open Level, Mixed Levels, Fundamentals
- Foreign-language ruleset added (ES/PT/IT — prophylactic)
- 'Intro to Tango' regex broadened to catch 'Intro Argentine Tango Class'
- No-partner-needed confirmed as friendly-only (not forBeginners) — Harvey corpus shows 26 hits where experienced dancers also benefit
- Edge-case table expanded with 9 confirmed resolutions

Q1, Q2, Q3 from v1 resolved. 5 open questions remain — most important for you:
- Q2: Beginners site — admit on either flag, or only forBeginners?
- Q3: Campaign segments — proposed 3, confirm or extend?
- Q4: Where classifier lives — calendar-be-af only (v1), harvester also (faster), or both (robust)?
- Q5: Fixture-based testing — Harvey is building 40-60 labeled rows; should the fixture live in Collab so all consumers validate against the same gold set?

Fulton (calendar-be-af)
